questions.q: Match the following List-I with List-II
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.solution:
(A)
$\begin{aligned}
& |a+i b||z|=|z||(a-1)+i b| \Rightarrow \frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}=\sqrt{(a-1)^2+b^2} \text { and } a^2+b^2=\frac{1}{2} \\
& \Rightarrow 1-2 a=0 \Rightarrow a=\frac{1}{2} \text { and } b^2=\frac{1}{4} \Rightarrow b=\frac{1}{2} \\
& \Rightarrow a-b=0
\end{aligned}$
(B) We must have either $6 a \gt 2^a \cdot 3^b$ or $6 b \gt 2^a, 3^b$
If $b=0$, then $6 a \gt 2^a \Rightarrow a=1.2,3,4$
If $\mathrm{a}=0$, then $6 \mathrm{~b} \gt 3^{\mathrm{b}} \Rightarrow \mathrm{b}=1,2$
suppose $\mathrm{a} \gt 0$ and $\mathrm{b} \gt 0 \Rightarrow$ if $6 \mathrm{a} \gt 2^{\mathrm{a}} \cdot 3^{\mathrm{b}}$
thus $6^a \gt 2^a .3$ so $2 a \gt 2^a$ (not possible)
similarly if $6 \mathrm{~b} \gt 2^{\mathrm{a}} .3^{\mathrm{b}}$ then $3 \mathrm{~b} \gt 3^{\mathrm{b}}$ not possible
$\Rightarrow$ only solutions are $2,4,8,16,3,9$
(C) $\operatorname{Re}\left(\frac{z}{4}\right) \in(0$.
1). $\operatorname{lm}\left(\frac{z}{4}\right) \in[0,1)$
means that if $z=a+i b$ than $a, b \in(0,4)$
Now $\frac{4}{a-i b}=\frac{4 a}{a^2+b^2}+\frac{4 b i}{a^2+b^2}$
$\Rightarrow 0 \lt \mathrm{a}, \mathrm{b} \lt \frac{\mathrm{a}^2+\mathrm{b}^2}{4}$
$\Rightarrow(\mathrm{a}-2)^2+\mathrm{b}^2 \gt 4 \text { and } \mathrm{a}^2+(\mathrm{b}-2)^2 \gt 4$
So we want area inside the square and outside the two circles
$\Rightarrow \text { Area }=16-4 \pi+(2 \pi-4)=12-2 \pi$
(D)
$\begin{aligned}
& 3 \mathrm{x}+4 \mathrm{y}+\hat{\mathrm{z}}=5 \\
& \text { let } \overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}}=3 \hat{\mathrm{i}}+4 \hat{\mathrm{j}}+\hat{\mathrm{k}} \\
& \overrightarrow{\mathrm{~b}}=x \hat{\mathrm{i}}+\mathrm{y} \hat{\mathrm{j}}+\mathrm{z} \hat{\mathrm{k}} \\
& \Rightarrow(\overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}} \cdot \overrightarrow{\mathrm{~b}}) \leq|\overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}}| \overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}} \mid \\
& \Rightarrow 5 \leq \sqrt{9+16+1} \cdot \sqrt{\mathrm{x}^2+\mathrm{y}^2+\mathrm{z}^2} \\
& \Rightarrow\left(\mathrm{x}^2+\mathrm{y}^2+\mathrm{z}^2\right) \geq \frac{25}{26}
\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.solution:
(a) $\rightarrow(\mathrm{P}, \mathrm{Q}, \mathrm{R})$;
(b) $\rightarrow(\mathrm{Q})$;
$(\mathrm{c}) \rightarrow(\mathrm{P})$
(A) Let $z=x+i y$
$$
\begin{aligned}
& \therefore \quad 2 x=2 \sqrt{(x-1)^2+y^2} \\
& \Rightarrow x^2=x^2-2 x+1+y^2 \quad \Rightarrow \quad y^2=2 x-1 \\
&
\end{aligned}
$$

If $z_1=x_1+i y_1$ and $z_2=x_2+i y_2$, then
$$
\begin{aligned}
& \quad y_1^2-y_2^2=2\left(x_1-x_2\right) \Rightarrow \frac{y_1+y_2}{2}=\frac{x_1-x_2}{y_1-y_2} \\
& \because \quad \arg \left(z_1-z_2\right)=\frac{\pi}{4} \Rightarrow \frac{y_1-y_2}{x_1-x_2}=1 \Rightarrow y_1+y_2=2 \\
& \therefore \quad \operatorname{Im}\left(z_1+z_2\right)=2
\end{aligned}
$$

(B) Let mid-point of $P Q$ be $(h, k)$
$\therefore \quad$ Equation of $P Q$ is $\frac{x k+y h}{2}-c^2=h k-c^2 \Rightarrow x k+y h=2 h k$
$\Rightarrow \quad y=\left(\frac{-k}{h}\right) x+2 k...(1)$
and equation of $P Q$ as tangent on $x^2=8 y$
$$
y=m x-2 m^2...(2)
$$
$\therefore \quad m=\frac{-k}{h}$ and $k=-m^2 \Rightarrow-k=\frac{k^2}{h^2}$
$$
\Rightarrow \quad x^2=-y
$$
$\therefore \quad$ Latus Rectum $=1$
(C) $\because D \leq 0 \Rightarrow 16-4 \log _{\left(\frac{1}{2}\right)} a \leq 0 \Rightarrow \log _{\left(\frac{1}{2}\right)} a \geq 4 \Rightarrow a \leq \frac{1}{16}$ and $a>0$
$\therefore \quad$ Number of integral values of $a=0$.
